definition: Distribution of traded volume at each price level over a period. Reveals where institutional orders concentrated (high volume) vs. where price moved freely (low volume).
depends_on: dealing_range (volume profile applied within dealing range context)
liquidity (volume profile reveals WHERE liquidity actually exists vs. theoretical)
feeds_into: draw_on_liquidity (low volume nodes are magnets — price accelerates through them)
related_to: liquidity_void (low volume areas in profile ARE liquidity voids)
order_blocks (high volume at OB zones CONFIRMS institutional activity)
fair_value_gaps (FVGs at low volume zones have highest fill probability)
bridge_integration: Volume profile is currently ASPIRATIONAL in the bridge — no real POC/VAH/VAL/HVN/LVN computation runs in the live signal path. (1) No detector imported: bridge/ict_pipeline.py imports from analysis.structure, analysis.fvg, analysis.order_blocks, analysis.liquidity, analysis.sessions, analysis.smt, analysis.ict.scorer, analysis.ict.core, analysis.ict.advanced. It does NOT import analysis.volume_profile, even though Desktop/trading-ai-v2/analysis/volume_profile.py implements a real bucketed POC/VAH/VAL/HVN/LVN engine (~30 buckets, 70% value-area expansion, HVN threshold avg×1.4, LVN threshold avg×0.5). That module is unused by the bridge. As a result, no SymbolAnalysis field carries POC, VAH, VAL, HVN, or LVN levels, and the Claude prompt is never told volume-profile coordinates. (2) Proxy synergy only: synergy_scorer.py:264-280 _ob_at_high_volume is the closest thing to volume-profile use in code — it approximates 'OB at HVN' by checking that ob_score >= 10 AND advanced_factors contains either 'FVG_stack' (from ict_pipeline.py:917 detect_fvg_stacks) or 'HiddenOB' (from ict_pipeline.py:958-965 detect_hidden_obs). The function's docstring at line 269 explicitly states 'This is an approximation since we don't have tick-level volume profile.' The 'OB at high-volume zone (institutional footprint confirmed)' synergy at synergy_scorer.py:449-454 awards +3 when this proxy fires — named tag 'OB+HVN'. The +3 is not actually a volume-profile signal, it is an FVG-stack-or-hidden-OB-coincides-with-OB signal that is LABELED as volume-profile for human reading. (3) No volume-profile prompt content: claude_decision.py _build_prompt has no volume-profile line. The 'volume_profile' card itself is also NOT auto-injected by concept_injector.py — there is no trigger condition (POC distance, HVN/LVN proximity, value-area boundary) that adds it to the up-to-8-concept prompt block. The card is reachable only via the dependency walker if a card that depends_on/feeds_into volume_profile is triggered, but most consuming cards (cross_correlations, conflict_resolution rules) reference volume_profile in text only and do not surface it to Claude. (4) Cross-validation rules in JSON not enforced: cross_correlations.json and conflict_resolution.json contain rules like 'OB at HVN = +5 conviction' and 'OB at LVN = reduce conviction' and 'FVG at LVN = high probability fill'. None of these are evaluated against real volume buckets — they reference fields the bridge does not compute. They live as documentation only. (5) Strategy catalog reference: bridge/strategy_knowledge/strategies_full_catalog.json:351-413 lists 'session_volume_profile', 'visible_range_volume_profile', 'volume_profile_lvn_poc' in indicator lists for some strategies. These are descriptive — they tell a human reader which TradingView indicators a strategy uses on the chart. They are NOT bridge-side detection hooks. (6) Wiring this in would require: (a) calling analysis.volume_profile.build_volume_profile(df) in ict_pipeline.py with an appropriate window (e.g. last N M15 bars or current session); (b) storing POC/VAH/VAL/HVN list/LVN list on SymbolAnalysis; (c) replacing the proxy in _ob_at_high_volume with real 'OB price overlaps an HVN bucket' check; (d) adding a prompt line + concept_injector hint when price is at POC/VAH/VAL or when entry/TP zones cross LVN/HVN. None of (a)-(d) currently exists. Until this work happens, treat any volume-profile claim Claude makes as unverified — the prompt does not give Claude real bucketed volume data, only the indirect FVG_stack/HiddenOB proxy.

key_levels.POC: Point of Control — the single price level with highest traded volume. Acts as strongest magnet. Price tends to return to POC.
key_levels.VAH: Value Area High — upper boundary of the 70% volume zone. Acts as resistance.
key_levels.VAL: Value Area Low — lower boundary of the 70% volume zone. Acts as support.
key_levels.HVN: High Volume Node — price level with concentrated volume. Acts as support/resistance (institutions defended this level).
key_levels.LVN: Low Volume Node — price level with minimal volume. Price accelerates THROUGH these zones (no one defending). LVN zones between entry and TP = price will move fast through them.

cross_validation: Volume profile VALIDATES other concepts: An OB at an HVN = confirmed institutional defense. An FVG at an LVN = high probability fill. An OB at an LVN = suspicious (no volume = no institutional activity).
